description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a distinctive pattern of alternating black and light gray horizontal stripes across its top surface. The stripes are evenly spaced and run parallel to the longer edge, giving the block a visually striking, almost industrial appearance. The sides of the block are solid black, contrasting sharply with the textured, marbled look of the lighter stripes on top. The block appears dense and heavy, with a flat, smooth upper surface suitable for supporting weight. There are no visible engravings, handles, or interactive features, and the block's edges are straight and well-defined. Its design suggests it is intended as a functional urban barrier or obstacle, possibly for use in public spaces or as part of a modern landscape feature.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with alternating black and marbled gray stripes on top and solid black sides, designed as a modern urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped top surface with horizontal black and gray bands and a solid black front face.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth of the block and the vertical alignment of the stripes, with the solid black side visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the striped top and solid black rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the block's thickness and the continuation of the striped pattern.
attribute_location_context: modern urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or placed directly on a leveled surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely placed as a standalone barrier or seating block
